text: I was a t mobile customer for more than 20 years. I cancelled my account in may got a bill in june, paid it  and then today get a collections agency saying i owe more money for charges for july thru sept, even their reps see that it was canceled in may  What a crooked company, i will never do business with them again plus tell everyone to stop using them.

While the customer service inside the store on Oracle in Tucson, AZ was great, its the customer service that is above them that is horrible.  They'll tell you you'll get a call from a supervisor in a certain time frame, you'll NEVER  get a call.  They'll keep you on hold while getting a supervisor, you're NEVER getting one.  Ultimately, the way I solved my problem was to go to Verizon.

Omg! I just left 290 & Northwest Freeway Cypress, Tx., after “feeling threatened” to a physical altercation with the Mgr.! 
I scheduled an appointment and arrived early. I had been waiting upfront for approximately 10 minutes, saw 4 employees, one employee showed up and started casually talking to a customer about an Apple Watch he bought, etc. He saw me standing there but never acknowledged me or much less welcomed me. I saw an advertisement of a Samsung phone on the wall & decided to take picture of ad so I can recall the phone, when the employee who could care less about my presence until then informed me I couldn’t take employee pictures! I was confused about his response, but said , “now you perk up”?  That alerted an employee (a substitute from another store) and she was so kind to hear my upsetting feelings to lack of customer service (no acknowledgment, wait time, no scheduled appointment station) & she went to mgr.  about my grievance. This was my second visit at same location with terrible customer service In less then a month. Previous scheduled appointment to activate new phone, I waited 30 minutes with no acknowledgement, question if I had an appointment, I asked “what was the purpose of scheduling appointments, if there were no stations to process those appointments “? She reported to mgr. “Angelica”. The Mgr. “Angelica” took offense of my complaints and she literally aggressively walked up to me and did not introduce herself and asked me “what’s your problem”? I was confused on what part of my problem she was asking about my scheduled appointment, or the terrible customer service & I’m thinking to myself “who are you?”
I repeated myself again about my asurion claim and how this store had terrible customer service with nobody acknowledging customers walking in and asking customers question like “do you have an appointment “? Her response was to ask another waiting customer how long he had been waiting & he responded “40 minutes”. 
I asked her if she was trying to help him now or listening to me? She said “I’m trying to tell you there others waiting”. My response was “that’s supposed to help me”? Because you could still acknowledge customers or new customers that are walking into your store “. If T-Mobiles adopts the philosophy
that we should be grateful to have them vs
“THEY”  should value their customers and remember they make their company successful or not! If Angelica’s mentality of not valuing all customers, is the new normal for Sprint customers ,  I’m afraid I have to leave “Sprint” after 15 years of service. 
I drove next door T-Mobile (doesn’t do repairs) to get phone number to call and had Roxanne @ Cypress, Tx.  location help me with my repair issue, & listened to my grievances. But , another customer was in there with same issue of terrible customer service at same location @hwy. 290 Cypress, Tx. Repair location. I believe the Mgr. has cultivated a terrible atmosphere of poor customer service and accountability is obviously lacking. Who comes up to customer confronting them about complaints?? Really?? I’m so sad because I have been with Sprint for so long! Dang it!!

I was a sprint customer since 8 years or more, recently I changed job that t-mobile BYOD plan was offered for my employer. I called to use that promotion, first they made me to create a new account in tmobile and transfer all of my numbers to tmobile (while sprint and tmobile were merged) . No! it wasn't easy I become a tennis ball between these two sprint and tmobile for days and hours. After couple of weeks trying different methods of calling sprint , calling tmobile, going to store, trying online I eventually made the transfer happen once I stayed in store for a whole day with the store rep trying to get someone to help the transfer by phone. After that the process of creating BYOD account was done and then transfering my old numbers from normal tmobile account to this BYOD account started. I have been calling them , everytime being transferred to multiple people and explain things over and over it is been 12 hours yesterday 4 hours, this morning 4 hours and another 4 hours on the call with them to make my phone number transfer from one account to another. Everytime they promise me it will be done in couple of hours and then it is not done yet!! very frustrated with the customer care and the whole organization!
no one reads the record of the other person, if the call gets disconnected no one calls me back! and they have been making me to go through the same process with a new set of reps again and wait on the line for 30 minutes every time.

An unbelievable amount of telemarketers constantly calling. My mother in law was on her last days on this earth and she was bombarded with telemarketers while on her death bed and by the time family would call, she was too mad to even answer the phone because she was bothered so much. I thought T Mobile was going to put a cap on how much they sell our info to marketing companies. Apparently they sell it on a daily basis and the calls will never stop.

I visited the Baxter Ave Store IN Louisville Ky this morning. Daniel went out of his way to listen to my problem. He clearly explained my options. When I returned with my SD card that I bought elsewhere, he gladly took my phone apart and installed the new SD card. He is very kind and knowledgeable. If I ever had any difficulty again I will see if I can find Daniel. Thanks Daniel!

Being more than 30 months of t-mobile customer, I discovered today that T-mobile is making fraud with their customers, i am one of their fool making customer. 

I got one device on EIP almost 15 months or more and i paid each month $37/- device EIP with Insurance $15 and Jump upgrade 6/- almost 58/- every month with tax all that, on September 22, 2020 i went to store to get jump upgrade deal with agreed price under jump upgrade i placed an order by paying tax they supposed  to send me new jump upgrade device by courier because that device was not available in store as per store staff and i also got sim in promotion by paying  $30 charges too and returned to home. 

An hour after reaching home i saw that store lady entered a wrong address of mine for shipping device because she was in front of screen so at that time I can't able see what she was typing on shipping despite I given her my ID, she entered a wrong address. 

Anyway, I found within an hour and call to customer service of t mobile to fix it, they said tracking number is not generated yet and we can fix by ups after that, next following day i called back to fix the address and after conference call they confirmed its fixed but will be one late to deliver instead of September 23 will be delivered on 24th, i said its ok, this process took 3 times conversation to fix address and activity is also seen on tracking number that address is corrected and will effect on day delivery blah blah. 

On Sept 24th late evening without any Information ups update the tracking details that package is send back to sender. I was surprised. 

Anyway, on Sept 25th morning I again called to t-mobile and Explained all, they said no problem we give you new device under same jump upgrade but you have to pay again tax and that one we will refund you, i still thought its okay but when she send me email its again trick to customer, she sent under regular upgrade which is on regular price. 

Right way I called back to canceled it, I asked them why would I buy under regular upgrade while I am paying every month jump upgrade extra for this benefit. They sent return label and said that one device in return transition mode once it will received in warehouse and the we can reschedule, as per them there was no other option, so I said okay then wait for that I the meanwhile wrong upgrade device received and same without anything we returned to ups, because ups courier guy never knock and bell at door he left outside the door which was also incredible job by ups if someone from outside door steal that device before we saw that I have many more torture I have bear on that too. 

Back to real under jump, since September 24th till today still it showing first never delivered device showing status send back to sender, in the meantime they said it went back by ground and take time.  

Today on October 5th, I decided to talk to supervisor despite so many conversations I was fade up on keeping on waiting i asked her, "Madam why should I suffer on waiting this is all your staff mistake and ups problem. She said you have no more option of jump upgrade we dropped the ball its canceled now, I was literally tormented and I said why should I be punished for wrong shipping address and she was insisting me to buy phone under regular upgrade of full price..I said why should I buy from regular upgrade but she clearly refused to give me jump upgrade. 

After spending so much time and effort I figure out today night at 3 clock because it keep me tormented that it really a fraud to customer by making this kind scenario to make them fool to not give their under juml upgarde because T-mobile will loose money in that..i want to request to all higher authority of t-mobile to look into this matter. 

Otherwise, It always be remains in my mind I have been cheated by T-mobile by grabbing every month my money under the name of jump upgarde and when time comes they play a game with me. 

I always prefer fair life dealing with each and everyone and by grace of God never did wrong to anyone. 

But this shows that clearly cheating or farud to every customer like me. 

Again please take an action on these types of cases may be there were several who quit from t-mobile because of no fair dealing with customer. 

Thank you

I am happy to say that I had an excellent experience at T-Mobile on US 19 N Palm Harbor. I am in the age of seniors and really do not have a good hold on all the updated phone perks that is offered now days. Hanisha Watson, the Retail Store Manager, was more than helpful in explaining what phone I needed to accommodate my needs and stay within my budget. She spent 1.5 hours without rushing me to assist with making the right decision. I am most grateful for her knowledge and guidance. Her reassurance to assist me in the future was very comforting. Thank you Hanisha...you are the best! Barbara Ruthberg

After many years with T-Mobile we switched to another carrier because they offered a much better deal on phones.

We paid what we thought was our final bill but to our surprise we received another bill for a full month even though we only used 5 days of service.

I called them and a supervisor told me that since we didn't stop our service at the end of the billing cycle and we ported our phone numbers out to another carrier they have to charge us a full month.

This should be illegal in my opinion and if nothing else tmobile should not brag about how great they are. 

We will NOT be returning to them as a customer.

I had to send back the tablet that wasn’t right for me and since I paid initial payment for this iPad I need my money back. 
Looks like the Refund process in TMobile is terrible. I am waiting already 2 months... 
So I was charged in 1 min, but refund will take 2 month or more, still not sure when I will get it back . 😐 So my advice Think Twice if you’re not sure you Really want  that or other type device or you will wait to get your money back  money long time and spend time on calls.
Definitely needs improvement.

i been with this !!!PCS!!! COMPANY FOR OVER FIVE YEARS!!! and all the years i been with this company i always have the same issue i do registere myself on the automatic payment and it work for like two months and then out of no where my service get interrupted for non payment, i pay and go back with the same exact card and get register while paying all the restoration fees. well has been a ride and to be honest the only reason i got stuck with this !!PCS!!company is because i travel for work and i do not got time to go to te store and deal with peaple that DO NOT KNOW ANYTHING ABOUT THE COMPANY OR THE SEERVICE. PAY ATTENTION I HAVE FRIENDS AND CO-WORKERS THAT ARE USING cRiKeT AND IT DO WORK PERFECT FOR THEM. And we do depend on our devices to do our jobs they never have complaing is kinda of hard to come up with the money for a device but believe me be pation and get that money together and go to a company like LIKE CRIKE Tyou will save money and you will have a hoSPot to share if you need to plus their service is great and the prices are sexy it will help. just think about having 300.00$ to put a side every month????????? DO NOT GET A CONTRACT WITH THIS !!!PCS!!! COMPANY IT WILL LEAVE YOU BROKE.

I got stuck with T mobile after the merge with sprint, as a result i have been dropping my signal all the time. I was told to go to the store an have them look at my phone only to find out from a T mobile employee that the sprint store was closed an I had to drive 40 miles to get my phone fixed. I told him that it's no excitable to make a customer drive that far only to be told to get another carrier an have a good day. T mobile stinks an I will get another carrier.

Although I don't have a T mobile account I needed a screen protector. Verizon wounded help me. Ronnie and Rob at the center Valley PA store were very helpful. They did my screen protector and actually helped me with my phone contacts which Verizon didn't. I had a great customer service experience.

tmobile is horrible. Do not do business with them. Read every contract. They will cheat you as a business or in your personal account. After 3 very long (hours) phone calls they are still unable/unwilling to fix account charges for items and services that were sold as complimentary. They took an iPhone as a trade in that they later said was not a trade in, they stole it and charged us for the new iPhone.

I have been with t-mobile for more than 5 years, last time I called to add a line to my plan they offered me a promotion which is galaxy Samsung phone and I have to pay the taxes only and which I accept it, after a month when I got my bill found out they added a new line in my plan without my approval and they start charging me for it and when I talked to a higher customer service (Tayler) he said I understand and we did this miscommunication with you  but you have to pay for the device and we don't accept the return back since it's been more than 20 days as our policy ! it's really funny the way it works with T-mobile ! they charge you whatever they want whenever they want ! also they add lines when they want not necessary your approval  but you will pay for it no matter what!!

T-Mobile wants so much for their phones that cannot be reliable on service you have a better chance at MetroPCS or boost or Walmart phones companies like this is horrible to charge people so much money for phones they can't even guarantee their service I get the same services I get with metro I am with T-Mobile thought I would get better service as the same as metro and I pay less for metro then I do with T-Mobile I have four lines on metro I pay $100 a month I went with T-Mobile get two phones and they want $100 a month plus the pay for the phones and still get bad service if you're wanting people to pay for your phone you should give them also the service they're paying for two should not come out with new phones if you cannot come out with great service maybe y'all should look into this

I am typically not the type of person to write reviews but my experience with T-Mobile recently has been absolutely horrible. This is more of a review of their costumer service, they have zero communication amongst one another and refuse to connect clients to upper management when issues are beyond the scope of their knowledge. To make things short, my phone broke and I had to order a new iPhone 11 online, which USPS ended up losing (no surprise there). I had to then go personally to a store where they told me they couldn't give me a new phone but instead I had to purchase the phone ($850) all over again and request a refund for the lost phone. I was fine until that point but then, days passed. I was told I would receive a refund soon, and nothing. I spoke with over 8 different agents and each time they would tell me wait 2 weeks, wait one more week, and so on. Eventually, over a month and half went by and they still did not send me a refund then had the audacity to request proof from my bank that I never received the funds from them, thus insinuating that I was lying. Ultimately, I had to go to WellsFargo and they were kind enough to start an investigation and placed the funds back in my account less than 10 days later. Days later a T-Mobile representative called my dad telling him the funds were refunded back to me, but they never were and WellsFargo had long done the process. Overall, this was a highly stressful experience, even more so because I am a med school student with decreased funds as is, and it just seemed extremely unprofessional on the company's part. Sadly, I am not the only one with negative views toward T-Mobile, a majority of people I've spoken to about this issue have agreed that the company provides subpar service.

4 months now I have tried to talk to T-Mobile's main office I'm 70 years old with physical issues sometimes I need to make a phone call from my house and even with Wi-Fi I cannot make a phone call leaving me hung out to dry with no way of getting help I have called and asked numerous times being that I've been with T-Mobile for at least 15 years and never owned a penny on my bill they keep giving me a bunch of bullcrap about giving me a phone helping me out giving me better technical service but never do I get a straight answer I have never seen a better bunch of liars in my life getting ready to turn everything over to Verizon because I borrowed a phone and it never lets me down anywhere I am T-Mobile is just a big company has enough employees and a business that they don't care about a 70 year old physically disabled individual they are terrible they have no compassion or concern for anyone I hope people see this and change their server

I have been with T-Mobile for over four years now. And I’ve purchased over four iPhones from them . I have eight lines for family members. And I have a fairly high monthly bill now . I went to pay it online as I normally do and my card was denied. And after several attempts I contacted T-Mobile. And after a very long and dragged out conversation with the so called expert I was told that I could only pay my bill with my bank account. That’s how you’re treated when you’ve been a good customer for over four years. I’m now a very unhappy and unsatisfied customer. ☹️

I have had a very bad experience with T-mobile. In fact I hate the day I change to T-mobile. If only I had investigated before the switch. T-mobile customer care agents are not there for you in fact their are there to make a sale/commissions and pretend they are with you every step of the way. I am talking about Carrier Freedom this is a  "Trick" to sound like a great deal but actually it's not. You may apply but hidden info would be left out. Please stay away from this promotion. You will never get the money and they will find small loop holes to say way you didn't get it. Well in my case I'm saying they spam me good. Now I owe my old carrier for a phone and T-mobile for their phone while my salesman sit on a commission from me (HAPPY). On top of it T-mobile has a recorded line/ documentation of our conversation from 7/2020 until present and they still would do nothing to help. I even tried calling  the Corporate Office. NO Luck...   You live and learn. They really don't deserve a one rating.You can rest assure I will be posting my experience on all social media so everyone can be aware of CARRIER FREEDOM. STAY AWAY!!!! If this has happen to you write a review let others know so they can stay clear.

Phone service fine but have a problem with the phone after a system update let to phone crippled. After having to go through the childhood steps to fix the phone it still isn't working and had to move on to the warranty replacement as it is new. I do have accident insurance too.  
Supposed to be 2 days shipping. The weekend rolls around and I call the store where it is supposed to be delivered. No phone and no help from the store.  No Help. Just says he doesn't have it and that is that. My sister tells me to be patient so I wait until today, 7 days after the ship they gave me.
The representative was very nice, don't get me wrong but you call me back an hour later telling me the phone is lost and they need to cancel the first order and ship another and you are not going to ship next day??  Not only that, it won't ship until next week. I am confused that they can't get a delivery on the weekend at the store but the rest of America can get it at their door.
I am sorry. I think they should have been more productive in following up after my first call and been embarrassed enough that they didn't research where the phone was over the weekend not just throw the blame on the delivery service. They don't share the tracking receipt like all other services do so we can see where are deliveries are going.
They should be sending this phone next day and to my house.  I am told that is not possible.  Frankly, it is. You just don't care about the customer enough. So much for being a loyal customer for 15 plus years... (I did just get a call back from the rep, she is good, saying that they could maybe have it Friday or Monday morning at the latest.  I still think this falls short given this day and age of technology and delivery services.)... It is now Monday 11:38 on and no phone still. No further notifications either.. the following Tuesday I called TM once again. I was told it was at the store. I went to the store they could not find it. It took the better part of 30 minutes for them to find it and then could not take the phone as out was never entered into their inventory. I had to leave and come back because it would take 30 minutes for it to get into inventory so that they could do my warranty exchange. A simple transaction that should have taken 30 minutes took about two hours. 🤔. As soon as it was entered into inventory I got an email that it was there. That notification is only good if everyone does their job correctly. I could have been waiting weeks of I waited for the system prompt...

Rip-off, do NOT go with T-Mobile!! After being told service would work at my location, and that I would get a refund if for some reason it didn't, I got the prepaid T-mobile plan. Service didn't work. Can't get more than 1 bar. I try to get a refund. I've called 7 or 8 times now, it's been over three weeks, and every time they put me off with some excuse and say I just need to "wait a bit longer". First they said 3-5 business days, then they said 7-10. Now they are hanging up on me when I call. I will NEVER do business with T-Mobile again, and I definitely will not recommend them.

***Update*** I received my refund. I contacted them by Facebook chat and customer service was much better. Chey F. jumped onto the issue, and actually figured out what was going on. It took another week and a half before the money reached my bank, but Chey went out of his way to keep checking in to let me know what was happening. There's still no excuse for what I had to go through before, but it did finally get resolved, thanks to Chey.

They don’t deserve 1 Star even, worst services ever, they wait for any opportunity to robbing your money and they always find a way to take your money, and the worst part is that you can’t do anything just to let them do it, because if no they send you to collection. Thanks god I change to AT&T, and I don’t want to know anything from this people again in my life, I don’t recommend to anybody T-mobile and MetroPcs now!

This customer service totally suck## they keep on transferring me to over .any fuckn## units ,   if you got people working with your company you should motivate them and train them to have the capacity to answer  all the questions just on one simple call instead of making your costumer waiting , we are busy amongst already with all this shi##happening

I've had tmobile for a year now. I wanted to switch before the merger from sprint. I actually had better service with sprint, tmobile has trouble getting data my phone is always buffering especially at work where my sprint phone worked fine, even at my house in the backyard it can't get a signal and my sprint had zero issues, another thing tmobile sends pics and attachments really slow and sometimes not at all, the customer service has been great, but the coverage is shi*ty. I signed up with att today which I had over a decade ago and the coverage was great,Verizon was the best, but the cost are way to high.

In December 2019 3 lines were on my account a representative suggested to save money move a line to become eligible for Magenta Plus..which forced my son to open a new account with separate billing $70 a month, but not treated as a new customer to receive benefits. Within a few days the same month and year Dec 2019 I became eligible for the Magenta Plan up to 3 lines. I always call in looking for ways to save MY MONEY.. Instead of saying, Ms. Bennett you can save by adding the line back on your account. 9 months later after spending $670+.. I feel like I was robbed. This country is in a Pandemic, people are unemployed, dying, facing evictions and the representative says I will give a credit of $60.. 😳 do the math. Who winning? Please do not insult me.. next offer $125 toward your next bill. 
additional insult. I would like the Regional Manager to review my account and tell me if I am a customer in excellent standing and how did whomever come up with the insulting credit. How about send a check. Both accounts are paid in full and on time each month. Put yourself in our position so you can realized the credit offered is totally unacceptable. The phone calls are recorded take time to care and listen. I trusted T-Mobile to give the best advice regarding phone plans. Will wait patiently on a more agreeable decision!
PBG

I had been a customer with T-mobile for about five years and in all that time, I never had any real big issues besides some bad reception in remote areas or not having any upgrade deals. The issues started when I was trying to switch mobile companies. The first issue was when I spoke to someone at T-mobile customer service over the phone. He was very nice at first, even sent me a text to get referrals for him, but as soon as I mentioned to him that I was calling to cancel my lines...his tone changed. Especially when I told him the company I was switching to (this was after he asked me what company). He rudely told me I was making a mistake and I would be paying way more with the new company. That was very unprofessional of him to make those remarks as he tried to guilt me in to staying. Another issue I had was I received a bill stating a negative balance. I chatted with someone at T-mobile and they assured me that I would be receiving a check in the mail. They gave me the number for the retention center in case I had any questions. I contacted the retention center for them to just tell me that there was no way of giving me a refund and nothing they could do due to their policy. What I don't understand is why was a bill sent if I was never going to get anything back? Both situations were and are still very frustrating and irritating because I was told two different things just to find out that nothing will be done. I am highly disappointed in T-mobile and how they treat their customers, especially those who have been with them as long as I had. I would NEVER refer anyone doing business with them.

Worst customer service ever. Total bait and switch. Promised a whole bunch of stuff. Phones arrived 1 week late. Porting over complete nightmare. Phone rep hung up because she couldn’t wait 5 minutes for me to pry daughters SIM card out. Texting customer service is a total joke. Can’t decide if it’s automated responses or they are just screwing  with me. Complete lack of any responsibility for shoddy unprofessional customer service. Returning phones tomorrow. I’m actually completely disappointed in what I thought I was getting. I was so excited a week ago.

I went to s.w.72nd and pacific hwy..... in Tigard Oregon. I was greeted by Dane Wrede a sales rep....  you need to know......  this rep was awesome!!! Knowledgeable friendly. Helpful...  he is what great service is all about!!!    I left with new cell phone.  A new tablet. Screens  and All my data moved to new machines 
Keep in mind I am mouthy..   a techie dork.....   he kept everything fun 
He should be HEAD OF CUSTOMER SERVICE
Pam

The worst company i have ever deal. Im in auto pay but they still disconnected my service every month and charging me $20 to reconnect and plus few dollars late fees. Im calling them every month for the same issue and what they are telling me is that there is not a problem with the auto pay. Last week they said that they will refund me the disconnect fees and i called them today because i did not get the refund and i found out that i have to make a payment of  $60  then they will refund me  $60. 
Run away from this company!

I had sprint since 2013 now that they merged with T Mobile. I had gone to a store a rep from there advised he couldn't do anything for me because he did not know the sprint programs for his store. He advised us to go home and call customer service. When i think he should of called customer service for us at the store. So everyone at this company could be on the same page. So I go home call customer service spoke to a manager Natalie. She sent me an e- Mail explaining how much of discounys we would get which was 500 dollars for each of our S8 phones. And that we can go to the west farms mall TMobile in Connecticut advised it would be a one stop shop they could fix the cracked screen so we could trade in our phones and get our upgrade phones. So we get to the store the store tells us there is no technician there today advised us to drive home and drive back at 430 because a technician would be there around then. The rep at the store advised my wife would only have to pay around 29 dollars for her small crack in her screen. We advised them we would give them the 29 dollars we would do the trade now amd they could have the technician fix it when he or she gets in. Tjey said they can't do that. Smh. I then drive home calling customer service at TMobile they advised there is nothing they can do without the screen not being fixed because we still wanted to trade our phones in and kust pay for them to fix the phone with the crack in it and they advised they couldn't do anything. Store manager said TMOBILES Policies phone needs to be fixed before trade in. Smh when the store has a trade in and a technician to fix phones but he was not there today. But that is the customers fault. We have to do all the driving and calling I would never recommend anyone ever get this service. Even the insurance plan gave us the run around. Tmobile was also pointing fingers at Sprint Sprint pointing fingers at Tmobile. Then they both began blaming samsung for our phones not working. It was the most rediculous experience I have ever faced. I have waisted a total of 10 hours with Tmobile from saturday to monday. I really hope the ceo understands how horrible of a job he is doing and how horrible his customer service reps are and there policies are garbage. The CEO just needs to get it together between stores and customer service reps on the phone. There is no communication between the 2. O ya i had also asked natalie if she could call the store where me and my wife were going in the mall and coordinate with them so they knew we were coming. She advised she could not do that. I beg everyone to stay far away it was one of the most stressful things me and my wife had ever dealt with. You will just get the run around. Stay away. TMOBILE IS NOTHING BUT GARBAGE AND IS HORRIBLE WITH CUSTOMER SERVICE.
Before T-Mobile and Sprint merged I've had a bad signal and now that they've merged w T-Mobile ITS WORSE . If I could give this phone company no stars i would . ABSOLUTELY HORRIBLE .
Online bill pay are trying to change plans is a nightmare

I've had T mobile 5 years. They are the best. Not only the best service, but they contributed in a BIG way to those in lockdown during this Covid pandemic at my husband's Nursing Home. The wifi was weak, and everyone there, all 300, were having problems from time to time with a weak wifi. T mobile placed a black box " booster" thingy in my husband's room, and everyone now has better wifi thanks to T mobile. Even those not using T mobile because it boosts the house wifi. NOW, people who never get visits from family due to quarantine get " face time" better and longer phone time to family they haven't visited since February ! Very important ! Thank you T Mobile for your contribution to the seniors during these harder times. And thank you for great phone service on my phone as well!

Since they merged the service and speed is pure garbage. Sprint was always an excellent service for us but now that these useless people have it nothing works the same. 
If you’re considering t mobile all I can say is run anywhere but here. They’re the worst company I’ve ever dealt with. Most of my apps that worked perfect and fast on sprint I now have to switch to 3g just to get them to run properly without an ( network error) popping up. 
Was grandfathered in to sprint with unlimited service now t mobile throttles it every time I get close to 30 gig. 
Once again save your money, time , and plenty of aggravating experiences and go anywhere else even straight talk

This is the last form of communication should serve as a warning to those that use business services or individual I really don't think there is a difference when it applies to the over all inequality. I sent to C/S T-Mobile: Face time and other limitations 
We’ve been unable to use as well as a host of features for our phone sets, iPad(s) and other for a substantially long time. It is still the same disconnect or the lack of problem solving on part of T-mobile. Unfortunately, we have detailed data to provide an accurate account of these unfortunate circumstances. There is a huge disconnect with t-mobile and its commercial account holders (My team) These experiences have left all of us indifferent with t-mobiles as a whole. Their reluctance to improve on these services are just unfortunate. Lucky for use we have the power of social media to express our concerns and experience. At the end of this we will have parted ways My team a lost customer account along with we’ll documented and thorough reviews.

I would rate 0 if I could. A few days ago we decided to look into t mobile and what they can offer us as new customers. We currently had att and felt like they continued to miss the mark on things they were promising us. anyways we got on the line with some man in Australia who promised us that t mobile would buy out our att lines and we would receive gift cards for each line we opened which would be 4 gift cards for us all we needed to do was pay $150 which we did. He told us he would send us new SIM cards and it was that easy. It sounded weird to me because I did not think it would be that easy however with COVID I thought maybe things were going different and I am not a technical person. With what he promised us it sounded like an amazing offer... to good to be true. The next day comes and we get our SIM cards and we try to pop them in and they don’t work. We get on the phone with t mobile and they failed to tell us that att needs to unlock our phones first before we can do all this. Att will not unlock our phones till they are paid off but when we go back to get t mobile to pay off the lines and follow up with what we were promised we CANNOT get  help! Somehow t mobile already ported our numbers over without even knowing whether they were unlocked or not. This caused us to lose service since the phones were not unlocked. We had to download apps to call with WiFi and asked T-Mobile to please do what they said they would however they just wanted to try and sell us new phones. No one knew how to pay off lines or anything like that. We got fed up and tried to go back to att which they were not pleased about since we just tried to leave and asked us to pay reinstatement fees etc. We did not want to do this to receive the service we were already getting so we got back on with T-Mobile and began a five day ordeal of trying to make them understand what is happened. No one understands, we are getting transferred from the Philippines then to India then back to the US and then from customer service to sales and back and every time we try to talk to a manager the call conveniently gets dropped. We don’t know what to do! We just want what was promised to us. No one offered any kind of help for all the confusion or any offers for what we’ve been through just the same lame commercial promos that are too expensive to start with.

Right down the toilet service went with this merger. Service no longer works. Paying a $600 a month bill for 7 devices that dont work. Asurion sucks too. They used to overnight new devices when you had an issue. Now they tell everyone they think it can be repaired please take to an authorized service center. They are either closed or extremely backed up and unhelpful due to covid. I will be terminating early and if they try to charge me I will sue them. Dont use!!

THE SERVICE ON T-MOVILE AND ASSURANT HAS BEEN A DISASTER, I HAVE BEEN CLAIMING MY PHONE FOR DAMAGE FOR MORE THAN A WEEK SEP.16 2020 AND IT HAS NOT BEEN POSSIBLE TO REPLACE MY DEVICE. 

ASSURANT COMPANY THAT SUPPOSEDLY ARE THE INSURANCE MANAGERS HAS BEEN A COMPLETE DISASTER, INOPERANT, POOR CUSTOMER SERVICE.
-5. Terrible!  It sucks.  What happened to Maps??????

Worst cell phone service on planet. 13 hrs on phone..visit to store..4 different expert techs..still no service. 60 dollars for sim cards...told to buy two more..then told to purchase tower forv25 dollars...three days later no service...then suggested to have phones re configured. Finally a expert tech told me I should have buyers remorse...tmoble is terrible and go back to att. After three days and 13 hrs on phone called att and was ported back over in 7 minutes!!! Will be contacting pa attorney General office over refused refund and the bbb. It is not buyers remorse over refund...it is fraud...cant sell me something you can't provide with hidden charges. I will not stop until refunded!!!!!

I've had t mobile for over 6 years and they never seem to let me down when it comes to issues with my plan, phone and or service. I've finally had enough and decided to share the final straw. I have a Samsung galaxy s20 ultra which from day one has not worked properly. My service is terrible, I have to constantly retry outgoing calls, incoming calls go straight to voice mail, text messages don't send, can't receive messages, and when I finally do get some type of decent service messages will come in 6+ times. I'll have full bars and no service, 5g signal but slow to no internet connection. Extremely frustrating paying for a 1500$ phone with cell service to use as a wifi only device. I have contacted t mobile numerous times to resolve the issue and over the past 8 months have been given different troubleshooting methods that never resolve the issue. Finally yesterday an associate tells me it's a software issue from Samsungs end, which is a little confusing to me, seeing how I never heard of these issues with other carriers on the same phone. They only offered to have me trade in my phone for a lesser phone. Or pay more money for a better one. Not taking into account that I paid about 600$ on this phone already. Which is extremely unfair to pay for a phone for so long that has not functioned properly. T mobile has given me so many issues I try to be understanding but this is it. I would not recommend T mobile to my worst enemy. Such a failure of a company. Taking so many steps backwards acquiring sprint who has the worst cell service out there even worse than budget services. Suck it T mobile!

I am trying to contact customer service in the Netherlands and there are NO contact details. The 611 number has been disconnected and the boot says that due to Corona they are not available and will be back to 24/7 contact shortly. Bot (Lotte) only writes back in Dutch. Really bad customer service.

I have been with Sprint for 11 years and the customer service has always been exceptional. As soon as the merge with T-mobile occurred, I wanted to upgrade my phone and had issues. I wanted to know why my down payment increased from $100 to $500. 

I spent a minimum of 8 hours on this issue-chatting with different sales reps. I was told initially I would need to pay off the old device- I did that. Then I was told I would need to wait 4 days. I did that. After being told basically "I don't know" by multiple CSRs, I chatted with someone who apologized saying all those people were wrong – I would need to wait until the end of my billing cycle- So as I waiting a month for the billing cycle to end so my credit would increase in the account, the phone color I wanted sold out. But I checked back at the end after patiently waiting a month and my down payment barely went down. I had the exact same phone, with the exact same plan, with the exact same amount of lines and credit score. There was no reason for the down payment to be different from that line. After hours and hours of explaining and re-explaining this issue, as I was passed to multiple different people "with more information" all telling me they couldn't help me- I said I just wanted an answer, not help- what factors have changed? Why is my down payment 4 times as much as it was? I finally got the answer- "credit policies have changed since we merged with T-mobile." 

I spent a minimum of 8 hours chatting and talking with people to get this answer. So just because T-mobile can claim "Over time, you will see additional benefits as we supercharge our network and honor our commitment to offer the same or better rate plans to our customers." and "T-Mobile has committed to delivering the same or better rate plans for at least three years—that includes access to 5G. Your whole family can continue using their smartphones the way they need without worrying about what you'll pay." does not mean that you will be getting the same great payment offers you have relied on for 11 years from Sprint. You will not be treated the same because they will tell you everything but “you are having this issue because we are a different company now” So hopefully if you are reading this, I saved you 8 hours. If you are from Sprint and expect the same anything- think again.

Very bad service, I stopped use them, and text them to turn account off.. message didn’t go through their app, i went to the store and their employees tell me to call support number.. i tried it and its impossible to reach them.. called like 5 times... so guys fyxkk that company !!!!! Never use that worst service ever in my life

These reviews should go negative. One star is too good for T-Mobil. I have been with them about 8 years, overpaying for 2 phones, although only one was in use. 6 months ago I switched to 1 phone only for the $50 new plan for seniors. Well, every month since I've had to call and wait for an hour after I get a text saying they will be doing an auto deduction from my bank in sums of $80-$93. So much for my $50 plan huh? After every call the "expert " tells me now it is fixed! Yeah right? I have had enough. Time to switch. I wonder how many other people this happens to on a monthly basis who don't catch it? T-mobile is a den of thieves. Don't fall for the misleading customer service ratings. They suck.

edits.text: I recently purchased a new phone(s) from T-Mobile as my old LG had died. I purchased a BOGO free on the LG4. I have been with T-Mobile for at least 5 years prior and have had issues with them in the past but this one takes the cake. I did the deal in store and walked out with a total of $85 a month thereabouts for everything that was not prepaid. Last months bill was %86, no issues. This month it is $92 and change? I called with my concern and was told all the numbers add up so you must be mistaken? I looked at my receipts and low and behold, no MENTION of the monthly payments! How can they sell phone plans and not give a receipt? Is this legal? Anyway, bad on me, fell for the bait and switch.

text: I have been with T-Mobile since 2009. My entire family left T-Mobile to other services. Due to my travel I stayed with T-Mobile. I pay $130 with the most advanced service they offer, and still does not work. Stay away from this service, this service is trash. Every call fails, no one can hear me, no internet service, o do not get calls nor missed calls. The merging of Sprint made it even worse. IT IS THE WORST SERVICE PROVIDER, can’t wait to join Verizon!!!
The Wifi is garbage

Since their merger it is a chaotic stinking mess! No one knows where lines are ported and they will not release lines unless you threaten them to get their act together!
They charge way too much for their garbage!

UPDATE : T-Mobile has fixed the problem in a timely manner. Not sure if this posting had anything to do with the fix, but it has been resolved. 

I am no longer a "fan". I recently had to cancel my postpaid account due to money issues. My bill was finalized on the 18th but received a call from a collection agency today the 21st before I even received my final bill. It has already shown up on my credit report and dropped my score by 67 points. What did T-Mobile customer service say? We have no idea how your information was already sent out to a collection agency. I was planning on coming back as a prepaid account since I spend most of my data on wifi, but that has changed. Goodbye T-Mobile. And corporate was no help either. Same excuse. A loyal 20 year customer and that is what I get for doing my best to defend them... Horrible.

why did the chat option disappear after I let them know I am a very dissatisfied customer! I had a conversation with a chat agent in which I disagreed about a service that I should have on my service! after I made known that I thought the service wasn’t as good as it should be. the chat option is no longer there! Why? It seems the merger is already affecting this company’s customers. I have been a Cust for more than 8 years and they would not make an allowance for something that was their mistake! I won’t stay around to be abused by a company that is suppose to be customer focused! Abusive for no reason! It’s ridiculous!

Here’s a horror story. Tmobile advertised a buy an IPhone get one for 100$ deal. In total it was going to cost up front 200$ then the rest was in payments applied to the monthly bill. Tmobile ended up charging my account in full $2,472 dollars! Bounced my account. Then when I told them of the mistake they said they would look into it and call me back. Never received a call back. My account is completely drained so I cannot buy groceries or daily necessities for my family. The next person I spoke to with Tmobile told me that I would have to wait until the next day until they could look into the problem. Meanwhile my bank account is locked up. On top of that the funds may not even be returned to my account for several days if not weeks.

Update: I received 4 phones in the mail when I only asked for two. Tmobile gave me two extra lines when I only needed one added. I have sent back the two phones priority mail to finally get my money back as that is the only way Tmobile said they would credit me. I even got tracking so I would know when they received them. Now its been a week since they had the phones back in their possession. It is now October 6, I have been talking to Tmobile everyday for hours trying to get these charges removed to no avail. I received  a letter in the mail yesterday stating that the “charges would not be removed that I still have the phones.” Lol this is just ridiculous now. I talked to them again and this agent apologized and said it would be taken care of. Meanwhile my bank account is still locked up. I smell a lawsuit brewing if this mistake Tmobile has made effects my review with the bank for my buisness floorplan. I feel like I am in a dream and can’t wake up.

T-Mobile is  a scam and their company and people who work for them have been overcharging me taking unauthorized payments that aren’t due and taking advantage of me I thought they would account for their mistakes instead raised my rates I’m now paying 112 dollars for 1 line the month of June to July was 258 does that make sense I don’t think so please people learn from me and go to a different company

ID number:187078 Joy F

ID Kevin #187546 

Customer service in
Manialla 

Were very rude and both said that they wouldn't mind me leaving as a customer and encouraged to leave since I my payment wasn't accepted which wasn't on my end it was on theirs, I repeatedly tried to make a payment on my account for 3 days, and it wasn't accepted and so now I am without services. Joy argued with me back an forth being were rude and interrupting me over and over when I said that wasn't great customer service she stated "She didn't care" I asked to speak with someone else and Kevin got on the line, Kevin was no help at all and encouraged me to leave tmobile. Tmobile is the worst. They can not satisfy there paying customer. What a disappointment.

T-mobile, after the BBB got involved, you finally attempted to make it right, the check got lost in the mail one more time, and you tried to short me again (you clearly don't have enough money, just ask Sprint) but all in all you finally corrected the mistake. So I'm changing you to a 3 star. 

I went to get my son's phone repaired at a independent service, and they could  not do it that day, right next door was T-Mobile. So we went in and we looked in to 2 new phones on 3 or 4 lines. I chose two phones that were both approx. $250. After hours like 3 hours and a card swipe of over $700 the clerk asks me if I wanted to put the 2nd phone on the leasing program. I was blown away, I said what did the $700 pay for and he mumbled some things with a heavy Spanish accent. I asked for the receipt and was told it would be emailed later. So I just left. Now we are signed up for service have only one phone, and have no idea what is going on. In order to see the receipt, you have to create an online account. I see there are employee assist charges, activation charges, deposits. I'm all the way out. I return under the 14 day policy and they give me all the money except the $180 deposit. That will come in the mail. well its been almost two months, no refund. So I call and they finally find the documents (which I can no longer access) and do a little math, apparently on one phone that was active we had to pay $70 for service, and the visa card has been mailed out. I never received it, ok, here's a number to Citibank to report it lost and go through that whole process. I literally give up, keep it T-Mobile. you are terrible

I am fourteen year T-Moble customer only because the cell service I need is excellent. Over the decade and a half as their customer, I have had less than a half dozen contacts with their "customer service." Unfortunately this year I changed cell phones and I remember the horror of those few times I had to contact T-Mobile.

I cannot describe "the service" received in their "stores," the language need would get me banned from yelp for life.

This may be my last year with T-Mobile.

I find it to be very slow and frustrating.  Never had problem before until this year and now I do not have much service. Constantly loosing my internet connection or takes too long to load so it times out. Other things it says Error,could not read, name not resolved,connection closed,your search timed out,connection refused,and a few more.
I'm sick of it. Actually I think of it as a "theft of service" I pay full price for something I do not get. The people in store are not much help either.
Another thing that bothers me is that Tmobile is anti-American and supports Marxist groups.

Update...... It all depends on which store you go to and who takes care of you.. The t-mobile on Soncy, the Mgr knows what she is doing.
 It was my phone the whole time. Seems to work good now.
But I'm still giving only two stars because of them being Anti American

We live in Pensacola FL... more precisely near Lillian AL on Perdido Bay... and were directly hit by Hurricane Sally. We lost all T-Mobile cell signal for 2 days, and now have very limited cell signal as of last night. Our neighbors with Verizon and AT&T never lost cell signal and can even stream video on their cell phones. WHY CAN’T WE? We were stranded by flooding and are still without electricity today. We were unable to contact family and friends by text or phone via our T-Mobile cell phones. I was with AT&T for over a decade before switching to T-Mobile last year. We NEVER lost cell service through several hurricanes with AT&T. When we were in danger of losing our lives to Hurricane Sally, we had no way to contact emergency services, friends, or family. T-Mobile put my family and myself at dire risk... and I will never forget or forgive T-Mobile for this. I am stuck with T-Mobile for now due to my large equipment contract, but rest assured... I AM SWITCHING WIRELESS PROVIDERS as soon as possible. I plan to warn everyone I know to avoid T-Mobile at all costs. —A VERY UNHAPPY CUSTOMER.

This is an update on my experience with this nightmare of a service.  Now, not only does the Bluetooth not work and the Internet connection is either extremely slow or nonexistent, but the phone itself is no longer reliable.  Half the time, it either there is an incomplete connection, or it just doesn't work at all.  My phone itself is fine, I've had it checked.  It's actually TMoble, and they have every excuse in the book for why this is happening.  However, they refuse to fix it.  I could get better service from a cheap track phone from a discount store.
Worst carrier ever. Their high speed data is really 3G speeds. DO NOT USE TMOBILE GO WITH ANY CARRIER BUT THEM. I’ll be switching to AT&T soon

I would like to give kudos to Stacy at store 3059. She is professional, respectful and knowledgeable about troubleshooting and solving the problems I had with my phone. She's truly an asset to your company.

The internet went out and they kept me on hold for so long to get it fixed that I was billed 30 euros. 
Then I canceled my plan but apparently still had an outsanding payment left. So I try to login to pay it but since my account was cancelled I cant pay it? But my name is on a debt collector list. 
I try contacting customer service but the bot just keeps sending me around in circles and they never let you talk to a real person.

I am a current Sprint customer. I have maxed out the devices on my plan (7) but wanted to add a tablet with SIM. Was told no by the Sprint rep. I asked why (my credit is good and income is there) She told me that this is something Tmobile set, and could not be overwritten. I asked how the determination was made. She said it has to with credit. I said that is impossible my credit file is locked and there is no pull showing on my credit file(s). I will wait for a credit denial letter from Tmobile within 10 days according to FCRA If I don't get a letter, then we intend to pursue in small claims court. So far this Spint - Tmobile merger has resulted in multiple outages for me, I have been getting bill credits between 80.00 - 150.00 monthly due to outages, Tmobile Tuesdays just suck (I mean a subscription to Dyrt?) and still no free Netflix as promised.

This company will tell you so many lies.  I miss Sprint.  My service is half of what it used to be.  I was lied to about buying a 30 dollar screen protector (its 8 on Amazon).  If you bought it there I was told if it cracked any t mobile would replace it.  After one month it cracked, I returned to the store I bought it at and they pretended to not know me.  I don't understand how you could forget the person you stared at for four hours while trying to navigate your own system to switch 4 phones from sprint to t mobile.  He then said you had to buy the insurance, something he omitted when I was in the store.  Customer service said they would make it right and send me a new cover.  On Aug 20 it was supposed to be shipped in 7 to 10 business days.  That was nearly a month ago.  When I called they checked the tracking information then said "do you want to order it again or just go to a store to pick it up?"  If that was an option why didn't we do it to start?  I then got disconnected and don't trust anything will happen.  No service, 5G phone, 1 G service.  Customer service a joke.

Beware of T-Mobile overcharging once you cancel the service. I was a longtime customer, always paying on time. I cancelled on 8/21, got charged for a whole month on 9/11. Before I switched, I contacted them twice (phone and echat) to verify I could switch without any fees, I was told no problem. Now TMobile claims they cannot issue a refund because my account no longer exists, however, they were able to charge my cc nonetheless. UNACCEPTABLE.

Terrible service in Georgia, and when you try to leave to get better service they make you wait 72 hours or 3 days to unlock your phone after you paid it off a month before!  I had 6 lines with T-Mobile, going to AT&T now.

HORRIBLE CRIMINALS!  They LIE about EVERYTHING just get a dollar.  Their rep STRAIGHT UP LIED to my face and said my phone would be unlocked (so I may use w/another carrier), as long as I paid off the ACCOUNT BALANCE.  She said she would have her manager expedite the request and have my phone unlocked in 2 hours and that she would call me back personally to see that is was unlocked.  She did call back, but the phone NEVER unlocked.  When I spoke to 2 different reps, they told me there's an EQUIPMENT balance (which the previous rep I spoke with NEVER mentioned) of nearly $400 that has to be paid before they will unlock the device.  T-mobile was already charging me $147/month for a SINGLE (ONE PERSON) phone line, so that tells you enough right there.  I just decided to switch data to the FREE NEW CELL PHONE furnished by AT&T (which offered me BETTER cell phone plan for $80 LESS and month than criminal T-Mobile.  GOOD RIDDANCE, I WILL NEVER EVER USE T-MOBILE SERVICES EVER AGAIN AND WILL MAKE SURE EVERYONE I KNOW DOES NOT OR DROPS THEM ASAP.
i noticed the same thing since  the  merger the service  has gotten much worse

edits.text: Recently the service of incompetence is what I get at tmobil wouldn't recommend them use to be good not anymore they give you a personal guarantee that's worthless

text: Honestly the worst phone company. I had Sprint and I needed to change my phone to Tmobile. The service worked for a few hours then I got no service whats so ever. I contacted their support team via chat stating I did not have service and I opened the plan less than 24 hours ago. After some time I messaged again and the member of the support team said he has been trying to call me but it just goes to voicemail... He said he couldn't help me. I went to the Tmobile store 3 separate times in 24 hours and no one could help me. They said it must be my phone and I needed to buy a new one because my "old" phone was a paperweight. No one could tell me anything. Worst customer service and somehow my phone was all of a sudden broken. I went to Verizon where the customer service is amazing and they were able to fix the issue and I switched companies. Tmobile is a waste of time.

I gave 1 star because they don’t have 0. Won’t answer the calls and if you go in the store they tell you it’s not their problem. Just trying to switch to another company and they won’t give up the original pin so I can switch over. I would suggest searching long and hard before using this company!!

I switched to T-Mobile about a year ago. It has never worked. Apple replaced my phone because they thought my lousy service was the phone. I visited the store at least 3 times and they acted like they didn't believe that my service rarely worked. They gave me a booster, tried everything but couldn't get it working. Finally I decided to switch to another provider that actually had service. What a nightmare. I payed off my phone so they'd unlock it. A week and hours by phone later they are still trying to figure out why it's not unlocked. They are the worst ever!

Switched from AT&T to T-mobile and absolutely hate it.  Horrible reception!  I live in a house in Denver so it's not their usual excuse that apartment buildings degrade the signal.  No exaggeration - half the time can't send or receive messages and phone calls get the message "No Service Available".  95% of the time 1 bar!  AT&T never less than 3.

It is a pain in the ass company, terrible people. I wait already 6 weeks for my TV channels. I waited 5 weeks on reconnecting/repair internet. Do urself a favor, forget them. Please Pls pls...(Den Haag Holland).

Don't Waste Your Time
Tmobile money is a startup joke. Customer service reps have no knowledge and will only give you the standard party line response because they don't really know. Bank mobile will terminate accounts without prior notification or reason claiming they can do that per their terms of service. Strange thing to do to an account that was open less than 24 hours and had no money transactions......
beware. There are other digital banking solutions out there that are better, Don't fall for the golden carrot they are dangling.....

T-Mobile isn't keeping up with its expansion or Promises of Great services, Unforgiving, God Awful customer Service if You can even understand them with the thick Accents and poor English...Login security loses PIN # then makes it near impossible to reconnect, I'm imagining they're botching services so customers will lose track of billing and incur extra fees and reconnect fees to boost the company's revenue...(a Shell Game) just a horrific experience all around...Verizon here I come...
Why did I change?? They suck! Worst decision ever. Stay away from this company!! Horrible service!!
Biggest mistake ever made going with T Mobile. Most complaints express my feelings

T-Mobile has always provided me with reliable service and great customer service. Now that the merge with Sprint has started I noticed I barely can service if any at all. I’ve stopped into my local store a few times thinking it might just be my new iPhone, however they tell me it’s because they are working on the towers. They have tried resetting my network location to ensure I’m pinging off the closest towers but that does absolutely nothing. After going into the store again today they told me this is going to take 3 years to update the towers... 3YEARS... I’m in the military and rely on my phone to stay in constant contact with my soldiers, and need the ability to do research from my phone on the go... I love the military plan I have with T-Mobile, but unfortunately I’m going to have to switch to Verizon. I need to be on a reliable network that I can trust to get the job done now, not 3 years from now

The WORST customer service experience BY FAR of any customer service I've had in 15 years. 

The problem goes all the way up to the leadership and systems. Shame for the executives running this company. I've had the representatives make multiple accidents to my account for 3 weeks: erasing active numbers of my account, leaving me and my family without service 3 times, adding add-on charges to the wrong numbers (after patiently confirming that their is only 1 add-on to one line), repeating the same mistakes back to back, and make false promises with no way to get back in contact with them. I have spoken with 10 different T-Mobile Experts. It has become evident that they do NOT take notes of problems with the account for other representatives, they go by the notifications of actions of the account. And the notifications DO NOT explain the problem. Or the solution. Or keep track of all the changes. It's incredible... The representatives will tell you they write notes, they do not. They also do not provide extension numbers or reference numbers. So as a customer you have to explain the story to each representative again and again and again. Worst of all, 9 of the 10 representatives I have spoken with, have only assumed what the problem was. They assure me that they know what happened. They do not listen.

I had to contact the FCC and BBB to get the help needed.  After 1 day of contacting them the t-mobiIes president office called me and gave me a refund of everything I paid out.  I had bad service from the 1st day of service, I contact the service reps at the 800 numbers but did not get help.  Had to call for 6 days straight, finally the service rep told me that it will be best if I cancel and also told me that I would get a refund and to take the item to the Durango, Co store in which this store has 2 sales staff who where the ones that lied to me and also did not honor the service reps promise.
this the worst app to use, they make you change your password every 3 months which is the dumbest thing in the world. i would not reccomend

Kudos and thanks to Ashley and Shala at store #40 who went above and beyond to help me set up my new phone and my husband's older phone. They were friendly and patient even when presented with obstacles. A good experience for a new T-mobile customer.
If I could give them 0 stars because they are a joke doesn't work most of the time t-mobile is the worst phone service I've ever had

Ever since the merger, my bill has been wrong every month.  They say Sprint has nothing to do with T-Mobile billing, I call BS.  It’s not no coincidence that all of a sudden there are billing issues.  After 14 years I have Never had any issues until now.  The dedicated team of experts once again has been outsourced.  U call and it’s a 45 minute wait, u elect a call back and get someone in the Philippines who don’t care.  They will research the issue and promise a call back, that never happens.  It’s very clear Sprint is taking over the billing and customer service aspect.  After 14 years, I left.

TMobile does not stand behind their unlimited screen protector guarantee. I have ordered a replacement screen protector 3 times and I still do not have a replacement.  When I call to speak with someone I got transferred to a supervisor or manager who never answered...VERY POOR CUSTOMER SERVICE! 
TMobile, your going to loose my business.

If I could leave a negative star rating I would. I had Sprint as my service provider, and up until the merger with T-Mobile, I had great service. Once the merger happened, I had horrible service. Dropped calls, inability to make calls, and Absolutely NO data service, plagued my wife and I (even in major cities...i.e. Atlanta). This is NOT a good time to be in a major US city without reliable phone service. I gave them 2 months. Over the course of which I spent speaking and chatting with their lying representatives saying the problem would be fixed, it never was! I was instructed to do the exact same things to fix the issues (even though they EVEN knew that wasn't the issue). If you are a Sprint customer run. I left for AT&T, great company, 5G service at my home, and peace of mind knowing that I can reach my family and vice versa. I also expressed concern that I relied on my phone to do my job, and that due to their service issues, I was in danger of losing my job. They did NOT care (maybe one representative, but she knew she couldn't fix the issue, so she sent me back to technical support where I had to repeat EVERYTHING...for nothing).

During the 2 month span of little to no service, There is no telling how many calls I missed. My wife showed me where she tried to call 5 times one night, but I showed her on my phone that none of those calls went through. Its unbelievable that they want to charge $213.00 a month, but deliver NO service. I was told by a representative that I could get 5G service for $270.00 p/month. That's absolute garbage when you've been with a company since 2014, and they want to thank you for being a "valued customer" by raising your bill that much. I even informed them I was leaving their service if there wasn't a service fix. They could care less, and acted like it was a joke. Given the 1.5 star review, I'm glad I'm not the only one feeling this way.

I have had t mobile for years.  However, the last 2 years their customer service has went down hill and the cell coverage is terrible now days. Then they took my out of account for 108.00. I call to find out why and they said my numbers ported same day the bill went out. I called them on that day and that Is when they told me I was paid to and clear. They told me on the 14th of next month and I might get my 108.00 back.
Why didn’t they credit my money back to my account today who knows. But they are going to hold my 108.00 hostage for 30 days,

I pay for a protection plan every month and my charging port is not working on my phone. They will not replace my phone unless I pay them $224 for a deductable. The protection plans are not worth paying for and the phones are junk. The repair shop told me I had a nik in the top of my phone that was not there when I dropped it off. I had a otter box case and no nicks when I dropped it off. Im highly frustrated. Go elsewhere!! Beware!!

Been a T-Mobile customer 10 years now and ever since T-Mobile merged with Sprint their data service has been absolutely horrible. Can rarely get enough signal or bandwidth to download anything or even open simple websites. Their data speeds used to be really good, but not anymore. Their customer service is not good either. Rarely can you get a representative that speaks English as their first language..if my wife and kids hadn't all gotten new phones I still have to pay on for another year, I would have already left T-Mobile and gone over to Verizon....choose any other carrier than T-Mobile!!!

I did some research in June and on the 30th went into a local store to replace phones that my sister and I had been using with another company for a little over a year.  We were not happy with the cheap almost obsolete phones that didn't cost us a thing and the reception.  (I live less than 1/2 mile from the T-Mobile store.)  What they told me my rates would be certainly beat the other company, plus they talked me into buying a 5g phone that was on special for 2 for 1.  My sister really couldn't handle a phone like that but they almost insisted that I take them up on that offer.  Now my second bill is $20 to $30 more than I was told it would be.  I called and said I wanted to pay off the charge for the 2 phones in order to lower my bill.  They said I had to pay the balance for 2 phones rather than the 2 for 1 price because I had signed up for a promotion that wouldn't end for several years!  Very disappointed in their billing support department mostly because they couldn't understand me at all, even when I was transferred to a supervisor who had very broken English.  There was also no way to lower my bill so here I sit with the bill going up and up every month.  Oh, and the super duper phones are doing exactly what the cheap no charge phones were doing.  I've called their Tech Support twice and they said there are bad signals coming thru but the only solution is to use 4g connection rather than wifi.  So I bought 5g phones but there's no 5g coverage in our area and I have to resort to the wifi to send anything in a text because T-Mobile's 5g that's really a 4g doesn't work.  What a mess!

This is the worst company I have ever dealt with. Number one the service is absolutely horrible. Number two the store stole my first 2 brand new phones I used as a upgrade when I came to T-Mobile and it took months to find them. In the mean time they had me using 2 junk phones until my new ones were supposed to come in. Needless to say I never received the phones I was supposed to get and was given a credit of 800. I paid 800 for the upgrade on the two phones and the phones I turned in were over 800 a piece. 😡. So now I upgraded my sons phone and it was lost somewhere I’m currently being charged for the new phone and the phone I mailed back and there is nothing I can do about it. They gave me a credit of $200 this time on the $800 I’m paying for the phone. It has taken them a year to find out the phone was lost and countless phone calls I’ve had to make to countless representatives. I’d advise anyone to stay away!!!!!

This our second try with T Mobile. I thought with all of the towers that came with their merger with Sprint the service might improve but alas I was wrong. I also didn't like the fact that they kept trying to push a survey down our throats before they even addressed our service issues. We just dropped them. Not worth the effort. Buyer Beware!

If you aren't helping the customer and your coverage sucks, what are you doing? Time to wake up leadership! This is your fault. If your people don't know what they are doing, you have failed. You should be ashamed of yourselves.
